name: Molasses Pumpkin Pie
recipeInstructions: For the crust, combine flour and salt. Cut shortening into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time, until the dough begins to come together. Form into a ball; wrap and chill for 15 minutes. Preheat oven to 375°F. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to fit into a 10-inch pie pan. Transfer to the pan; trim and flute edges. Reroll any scraps to 1/8-inch thick. Cut into leaves. Place on an ungreased baking sheet, cover, and refrigerate. For the filling, beat together pumpkin, evaporated milk, sugar, molasses, rum, eggs, spice, and salt. Pour into the crust. Bake for 55-60 minutes until the custard is set but the center jiggles slightly. Cool for 1 hour, then chill for 4 hours. Meanwhile, bake the leaves for 12 minutes or until lightly browned. Decorate the pie with leaves before serving. Makes 10 servings. Per serving: 300 calories, 6g protein, 11g fat, 44mg cholesterol, 41g carbohydrates, 270mg sodium. Source: Woman's World, November 1995. Formatted by Mary Wilson, BWVB02B.
